cards.back: A sorting algorithm that selects the smallest (or largest) element from the unsorted portion of the array and swaps it with the first unsorted element.
A sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.
O(n^2) in all cases (best, average, worst)
O(n^2) in a worst case scenario, but O(n) in the best case (when the list is already sorted)
These algorithms try all possible solutions to a problem, resulting in high computational complexity and inefficiency
